div1.entryFree:
Seizure, 1) the act of seizing, grasp, clasp: and with her lips on his did act the s. Pilgr. 152. shall these hands, so newly joined in love, unyoke this s.? John III, 1, 241. her hand, to whose soft s. the cygnet's down is harsh, Troil. I, 1, 57.
2) the act of taking possession by force: all things that thou dost call thine worth s. As III, 1, 10.
